steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with "Compared to that of the direct imaging method," — a comparison that requires the blank to supply a grammatical main clause, followed by a phrase that ends in "the effect these exoplanets have on nearby stars." The blank must (1) name the radial velocity method's zone of discovery and (2) set up a dependent clause describing how detection works.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the detection of distant exoplanets, too dim to be observed in the radial velocity method's vast zone of discovery, is based on': the subject "the detection" misplaces "too dim to be observed" inside the zone, contradicting the passage (dim exoplanets are detected by the radial velocity method, not excluded from it). ✗
  • Choice B — 'the radial velocity method's zone of discovery is vast, as distant exoplanets too dim to be observed are detected by': forms a clear independent clause ("the zone of discovery is vast") followed by "as" introducing a dependent clause that correctly ends with "the effect these exoplanets have on nearby stars." ✓
  • Choice C — 'the radial velocity method, with its vast zone of discovery, detects exoplanets that are too dim to be observed by': grammatically sound but "detects exoplanets... by the effect these exoplanets have on nearby stars" is circular and changes the meaning; the method detects via stellar effects, not "by the effect these exoplanets have." ✗
  • Choice D — 'distant exoplanets too dim to be observed are detected within the radial velocity method's vast zone of discovery through': no verb in a main clause that pairs with the opening comparison; "through the effect these exoplanets have on nearby stars" is ambiguous and produces an awkward run-on. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
